title: Eddy — fluid studio
description: A real-time, from-scratch incompressible Navier–Stokes fluid studio in pure TypeScript. Stable Fluids with semi-Lagrangian + MacCormack advection and FOUR pressure-projection Poisson solvers over one identical Neumann/obstacle stencil — red-black SOR, matrix-free Jacobi-preconditioned Conjugate Gradients, a from-scratch geometric MULTIGRID V-cycle (work-optimal O(N), with a convergence factor independent of grid size), and MULTIGRID-PRECONDITIONED CG (MGCG) that reaches machine-level residual in a fixed handful of iterations regardless of resolution and stays robust around obstacles. Plus vorticity confinement, a Boussinesq temperature field driving real thermal convection, a reactive combustion model (an advected fuel field that ignites, releases heat and burns down) with variable-density buoyancy, RGB ink, and arbitrary solid obstacles. Fourteen scenes — von Kármán vortex street, Rayleigh–Bénard convection, a buoyant plume, Kelvin–Helmholtz shear, the lid-driven-cavity benchmark, a self-sustaining Fire, the Taylor–Green vortex (an exact NS solution), decaying 2-D turbulence, and the double-shear-layer benchmark — plus a live SPECTRA LAB that runs a from-scratch 2-D FFT to plot the kinetic-energy spectrum E(k) and its k^-3 cascade, dye/speed/vorticity/pressure/temperature views, animated Line Integral Convolution, schlieren and Q-criterion vortex-core flow visualisation, streamline + tracer-particle overlays, a live hover probe, WebM capture, energy/divergence diagnostics, and a 34-check numerical verification suite (incl. grid-independent multigrid convergence, MGCG, an analytic diffusion-decay rate, and FFT/Parseval identities) that proves the solver's claims live in the browser.
